name: Santeria Magic
description: Santeria, also known as La Regla Lucumi, is an Afro-Cuban religion and magic system that syncretizes Yoruba beliefs with elements of Catholicism. It originated in Cuba among enslaved Yoruba people, who disguised their Orisha worship behind Catholic saints. Santeria magic focuses on interacting with the Orishas, powerful deities who govern different aspects of nature and human life. Practitioners use divination, offerings, rituals, and prayers to communicate with and request assistance from the Orishas. Divination methods include cowrie shell readings (Diloggun) and coconut readings (Obi). Offerings often consist of food, animals, and other items sacred to the specific Orisha. Santeria emphasizes a personal relationship with the Orishas and a commitment to living in harmony with nature and the divine. It is a complex and deeply rooted cultural tradition that continues to thrive in Cuba and throughout the diaspora, providing spiritual guidance, healing, and a connection to ancestral heritage. The religion emphasizes ethical behavior, community, and the pursuit of personal and spiritual growth through the guidance of the Orishas and the ancestors.
subtitle: Afro-Cuban Orisha worship and magic.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Olodumare, the supreme being, entrusted Obatala with the task of creating the world, but Obatala became drunk and messed up the creation, leading to physical imperfections in humans. Olodumare then sent Oduduwa to finish the creation.
keyMyths.source: Various oral traditions and written texts.

keyMyths.title: Oshun's Beauty and Power
keyMyths.description: Oshun, the Orisha of love, beauty, and rivers, used her feminine wiles and intelligence to save the world when the male Orishas failed to accomplish a task. This highlights her importance and power within the Santeria pantheon.
keyMyths.source: Various oral traditions and written texts.
